candidates.description: Add streak tracking (current and longest consecutive coding days)
candidates.rationale: Wakapi prominently displays current streak and longest streak as key engagement metrics. AI-Coding-Observability tracks daily sessions but doesn't compute streaks — a highly motivating metric for engineers.
candidates.key_insight: Compute consecutive-day streaks from session dates. Add current_streak_days and longest_streak_days to EfficiencyMetrics and display in the terminal report.

candidates.description: Add engagement tier scoring with color-coded levels
candidates.rationale: Copilot dashboard uses a 5-tier adoption scoring system (0-40% red through 95-100% blue) to categorize user engagement. The project already computes efficiency_score but doesn't map it to named tiers.
candidates.key_insight: Map efficiency_score to named engagement tiers with ANSI colors: Needs Attention (0-0.3), Developing (0.3-0.5), Productive (0.5-0.7), Strong (0.7-0.9), Power User (0.9-1.0).

candidates.description: Add ASCII contribution heatmap to terminal report
candidates.rationale: GitHubPoster generates GitHub-style contribution heatmaps from various data sources. An ASCII version in the terminal report would give engineers an immediate visual of their AI usage patterns across days.
candidates.key_insight: Render a compact 7-row (Mon-Sun) x N-week ASCII heatmap using Unicode block characters at varying intensities, showing daily active time.

candidates.description: Add week-over-week trend indicators to report
candidates.rationale: Copilot dashboard shows daily/weekly/monthly trend lines for all key metrics. The project only shows totals for the date range. Adding simple trend arrows (up/down/flat) would show momentum.
candidates.key_insight: Split the reporting date range in half, compare totals, and display trend arrows (up/down/flat) next to key metrics like total active time and coding percentage.

candidates.description: Add top-project leaderboard ranked by active time
candidates.rationale: Copilot dashboard has Top 10 user leaderboards with ranked bar charts. The project shows per-project breakdowns but doesn't rank them. A ranked mini-leaderboard adds quick scan value.
candidates.key_insight: Sort projects by active time descending and display as a ranked list with proportional bars in the terminal report summary.
